title: Number of 1 Bits
pattern: Bit Manipulation â€” Brian Kernighan's Algorithm
coreIdea: Count the number of set bits (1s) in an integer's binary representation by repeatedly clearing the lowest set bit using n & (n-1), which removes exactly one 1-bit per operation, counting iterations until n becomes 0.
recognitionCues: asks to count set bits / population count of a number
efficient solution expected over checking each of 32 bits individually
approach: Initialize count to 0. While n is not 0, do n = n & (n-1) (clears the lowest set bit) and increment count. Return count once n becomes 0.
timeComplexity: O(number of set bits), at most O(32)
